name: total-recall
description: Persistent tiered memory plugin for Claude Code. Four-tier storage: CLAUDE.local.md (auto-loaded counter), memory/registers/ (categorized persistent notes), memory/daily/ (timestamped scratch), memory/archive/ (searchable history). Includes SessionStart and PreCompact hooks plus slash commands (/recall-write, /recall-promote, /recall-search).

findings_summary.notes: Clean, focused memory plugin with well-written shell hooks. SessionStart hook reads memory files from project directory and injects context — no network calls, no credential access. PreCompact hook writes a compaction marker to daily log — benign. Install.sh copies .claude/commands/, .claude/rules/, and creates memory directory structure — standard and safe. The hooks use PROJECT_ROOT=${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)} pattern safely. No dangerous patterns (no eval, no curl during runtime, no external network access). Concept is clear and well-explained in README. Concern: no license file (license field is empty) — this is a legal ambiguity that means the code is technically all-rights-reserved by default. Install method via plugin marketplace (davegoldblatt/recall-marketplace) is slightly different from the GitHub repo name. Created Feb 2026, 8 forks, 2 open issues — relatively new but clean.
